headline: Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah offers prayers at the Bhagwan Ayyappa Temple in New Delhi
fullText:
Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah offers prayers at the Bhagwan Ayyappa Temple in New Delhi Bhagwan Ayyappa is the bestower of joy and the embodiment of the sacred values that unite our nationThe Home Minister inaugurated the newly constructed SABAREESAM Complex in the temple premisesThis new facility will serve as a hub for the spiritual discourses and welfare initiatives conducted by the templeIt will further strengthen the cultural roots of our brothers and sisters from Kerala

Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah today offered prayers at the Bhagwan Ayyappa Temple in New Delhi and inaugurated the newly constructed SABAREESAM Complex in the temple premises.

In a post on X, Union Home Minister, Shri Amit Shah said, "Fortunate to offer puja at Bhagwan Ayyappa temple, in R.K. Puram, New Delhi. Bhagwan Ayyappa is the bestower of bliss and the emodiment of the sacred values that unites our nation. Prayed to the deity for the well-being and prosperity of all.”

In another post, Shri Amit Shah said, “Today, at the Ayyappa Temple premises in New Delhi, inaugurated the newly built SABAREESAM Complex. The new facility will house spiritual discourses and welfare initiatives undertaken by the temple and strengthen the cultural roots of our sisters and brothers from Keralam.”

summaryText:
### **UPSC Study Note: Inauguration of SABAREESAM Complex**

**1. CONTEXT**
The Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation, Shri Amit Shah, visited the Bhagwan Ayyappa Temple in R.K. Puram, New Delhi, to offer prayers and officially inaugurate the newly constructed **SABAREESAM Complex** within the temple premises.

**2. KEY FEATURES**
*   **Purpose:** The complex is designed to function as a dedicated hub for hosting **spiritual discourses** and implementing various **welfare initiatives** led by the temple administration.
*   **Cultural Focus:** It aims to provide a structured space for the preservation and promotion of the traditions associated with Lord Ayyappa.
*   **Community Outreach:** The facility is specifically intended to strengthen the cultural and spiritual roots of the Malayali diaspora (people from Kerala) residing in the national capital.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE**
*   **Cultural Integration:** By promoting regional spiritual traditions in the capital, the initiative fosters the idea of *"Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat,"* bridging the cultural gap between South and North India.
*   **Social Welfare:** The integration of welfare initiatives within a spiritual center underscores the role of religious institutions in community service and social upliftment.
*   **National Unity:** The visit highlights the embodiment of sacred values that serve as a unifying force across diverse linguistic and regional boundaries.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE**
*   **GS Paper I (Indian Heritage & Culture):** Relevant for the study of regional religious traditions and the role of temples as centers of cultural preservation.
*   **GS Paper II (Governance):** Focus on the intersection of cultural diplomacy and social welfare initiatives.
*   **Keywords for Mains:** *Cultural Pluralism, Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat, Spiritual Diplomacy, Socio-cultural Integration.*

headline: PUBLIC PARTICIPATION, ENTREPRENEURSHIP, AND INNOVATION ARE KEY TO VIKSIT BHARAT 2047: LOK SABHA SPEAKER
fullText:
PUBLIC PARTICIPATION, ENTREPRENEURSHIP, AND INNOVATION ARE KEY TO VIKSIT BHARAT 2047: LOK SABHA SPEAKER ​LOK SABHA SPEAKER CALLS UPON YOUTH AND ENTREPRENEURS TO LEAD INDIA’S JOURNEY TOWARDS A DEVELOPED NATIONMAHESHWARI COMMUNITY’S LEGACY OF SERVICE, ETHICS, AND ENTERPRISE STRENGTHENS NATION-BUILDING: LOK SABHA SPEAKERENTREPRENEURSHIP AND NATION-BUILDING MUST PROGRESS TOGETHER, ASSERTS SPEAKER​LOK SABHA SPEAKER ADDRESSES PARTICIPANTS AT THE EVENT "MAHESHWARIS & THE MAKING OF VIKSIT BHARAT 2047

Lok Sabha Speaker Shri Om Birla today asserted that the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047 can only be realized through public participation, innovation, entrepreneurship, and collective resolve. Addressing on the theme 'Maheshwaris & Making of Viksit Bharat 2047' at National Conclave of the Maheshwari Community in New Delhi, he emphasized that entrepreneurship and nation-building must progress hand in hand to ensure that the benefits of development reach every section of society. He noted that India’s journey toward becoming a developed nation must be guided by inclusive growth, social responsibility, and active citizen engagement.

Describing Maheshwari community as a vibrant force in India's economic and social landscape, Shri Birla lauded its invaluable contributions to nation-building through service, ethical business practices, philanthropy, and social responsibility. He observed that the community has consistently worked for societal welfare by establishing and nurturing institutions dedicated to education, healthcare, and public service across generations and regions.

​Lok Sabha Speaker remarked that the Maheshwari community has historically demonstrated remarkable adaptability and enterprise, significantly driving regional economic transformations. Whether in metropolitan centers, the North-East, or southern India, community members have created opportunities, generated employment, and strengthened local economies. He added that the community’s commitment to honesty, ethical conduct, and social harmony has set exemplary standards in the business world.

​Shri Birla emphasized that the true measure of success is not wealth creation alone, but the generation of value and positive social change. Appreciating the entrepreneurial achievements of Shri Anand Rathi, he noted that his exemplary journey serves as an inspiration for generations of young entrepreneurs. He also lauded the contributions of Justice J.K. Maheshwari, observing that his efforts have played an important role in strengthening the judicial system and advancing legal reforms. Such distinguished personalities, he remarked, reflect the community’s deep-rooted tradition of excellence, leadership, and public service.

​Highlighting the role of youth in shaping the future, Shri Birla stated that educated, skilled, and technology-enabled young people constitute the greatest strength of Viksit Bharat 2047. He urged the younger generation to embrace innovation, artificial intelligence, and emerging technologies to position India as a global leader. He observed that the coming era will be driven by knowledge and innovation, calling upon the youth to become job creators rather than job seekers.

​The Speaker further noted that India's progress over the past decade has demonstrated the nation's resilience, self-confidence, and collective determination. He highlighted the importance of balancing rights with duties, stressing that responsible citizenship, respect for institutions, and active participation in public life are essential to sustain national development. Legislative reforms undertaken through Parliament and democratic institutions, he added, have significantly enhanced transparency and improved the ease of doing business.

​Concluding his address, Shri Birla expressed confidence that the spirit of unity, service, enterprise, and the principle of 'Nation First' will enable India to achieve the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047. He called upon the Maheshwari community, particularly its youth, to continue driving national development through innovation, ethical leadership, and social commitment. Shri Birla reaffirmed that a developed India can only be built when every citizen becomes an active participant in progress, and when economic growth goes hand in hand with social responsibility and collective welfare.

**2. KEY FEATURES:**
*   **Core Pillars:** Viksit Bharat 2047 hinges on active public participation, robust entrepreneurship, and continuous innovation.
*   **Inclusive Growth:** Development must ensure benefits reach all sections, guided by social responsibility and ethical conduct.
*   **Community Role:** Highlighted the Maheshwari community's legacy of enterprise, philanthropy, education, healthcare, and ethical business practices as a model for nation-building.
*   **Youth Imperative:** Urged educated, skilled youth to leverage AI and emerging technologies to become job creators and global leaders.
*   **Balanced Citizenship:** Stressed the need for citizens to balance rights with duties, respect institutions, and participate actively in public life.

**3. SIGNIFICANCE:**
*   Reinforces that nation-building requires a societal movement, not just government action.
*   Positions entrepreneurship and innovation as engines for inclusive economic growth and employment generation.
*   Validates the contributions of specific communities (like Maheshwaris) as vital to national progress.
*   Emphasizes the demographic dividend and the crucial role of youth as agents of change.

**4. UPSC RELEVANCE:**
*   **GS-3 (Economy):** Directly relates to entrepreneurship, ease of doing business, inclusive development, and leveraging technology for growth.
*   **GS-2 (Governance):** Addresses citizen participation, role of Parliament, responsible citizenship, and ethical governance.
*   **GS-1 (Society):** Highlights community contributions, social harmony, and the demographic dividend's potential.

headline: Ministry of Panchayati Raj to organise outreach workshop on Atmanirbhar Panchayat Programme at Gandhinagar, Gujarat on 9 June 2026
fullText:
Ministry of Panchayati Raj

Ministry of Panchayati Raj to organise outreach workshop on Atmanirbhar Panchayat Programme at Gandhinagar, Gujarat on 9 June 2026 Workshop to be held in hybrid mode, connecting eligible Gram Panchayats and Block Panchayats across Gujarat through video conferencing

The Ministry of Panchayati Raj, Government of India, is organising an Outreach Workshop on the Atmanirbhar Panchayat Programme on 9 June 2026 at Gandhinagar, Gujarat. The workshop will be held in the presence of Shri Vivek Bharadwaj, Secretary, Ministry of Panchayati Raj, Government of India; Smt. Mukta Shekhar, Joint Secretary, Ministry of Panchayati Raj; Shri Dhananjay Dwivedi, Principal Secretary, Panchayat, Rural Housing and Rural Development Department, Government of Gujarat, and Dr. Gaurav Dahiya, Additional Development Commissioner, Government of Gujarat along with senior officers of the State Government and representatives of Housing and Urban Development Corporation Limited (HUDCO) and National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) and Panchayat representatives and functionaries.

The workshop will be held in hybrid mode. Eligible Panchayats located around Gandhinagar will participate in person, while those beyond that radius will join the proceedings live through video conferencing from their respective offices. Through this outreach, the Ministry aims to directly engage with Gram Panchayat and Block Panchayat-level leaders across Gujarat and present to them the vision, objectives and operational framework of the Atmanirbhar Panchayat Programme.

The Workshop programme includes an address on the vision of the Atmanirbhar Panchayat Programme by the Secretary, Ministry of Panchayati Raj, a detailed presentation on the national policy objectives, technical roadmap and expected outcomes, a live end-to-end demonstration of the Atmanirbhar Panchayat Portal, and an interactive Question and Answer session. Representatives of NABARD and HUDCO will also address participants, reaffirming their institutional commitment to the programme.

The Atmanirbhar Panchayat Programme is a Government of India initiative anchored by the Ministry of Panchayati Raj, designed to promote the financial self-reliance of Panchayats. Through a transparent national challenge process, Panchayats are invited to submit ideas for revenue-generating projects and initiatives that put idle assets and untapped local opportunities to productive use. Selected ideas will receive dedicated technical assistance from the Ministry to develop them into feasible, bankable projects, with funding support drawn from Public-Private Partnerships (PPP), Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) contributions, bank finance and convergence with other government schemes. Community ownership is ensured through mandatory participation of the Gram Sabha at every stage of planning.

Gram Panchayats with a minimum Own Source Revenue (OSR) of Rs.50 lakh and Block Panchayats with a minimum OSR of Rs.1 crore, both with at least three years of tenure remaining, are eligible to participate in the challenge process. The Ministry of Panchayati Raj has partnered with NABARD and HUDCO to provide institutional expertise in rural project development and to facilitate financial support to qualifying Panchayats.

The Atmanirbhar Panchayat Programme is implemented under the Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA). It envisages to build a new generation of self-reliant, economically confident Panchayats, each a demonstration that financial independence and good local governance go hand in hand. In the vision of the Ministry, an Atmanirbhar Panchayat is the surest foundation of an Atmanirbhar Bharat. The Gandhinagar workshop marks the commencement of the programme’s outreach in the State of Gujarat.

Read this release in: Gujarati , हिन्दी
summaryText:
**1. Context**  
The Ministry of Panchayati Raj will hold an Outreach Workshop on the Atmanirbhar Panchayat Programme on 9 June 2026 in Gandhinagar, Gujarat. The hybrid event will have nearby Gram and Block Panchayats attend in person, while others join via video‑conference. Senior officials from the Ministry, Gujarat government, NABARD, HUDCO and Panchayat reps will be present.  

**2. Key Features**  
- **Eligibility:** Gram Panchayats with ≥ Rs 50  lakh Own Source Revenue (OSR) and Block Panchayats with ≥ Rs 1 crore OSR, each with at least three years of tenure remaining.  
- **Challenge Process:** Panchayats submit ideas for revenue‑generating projects using idle assets; selected ideas receive technical support and funding through PPP, CSR, bank finance and scheme convergence.  
- **Community Ownership:** Gram Sabha must participate at every planning stage.  
- **Implementation:** Under the Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA) and accessed via the Atmanirbhar Panchayat Portal.  

**3. Significance**  
The programme aims to make Panchayats financially self‑reliant, turning them into models of good governance that reinforce the broader goal of an Atmanirbhar Bharat. It strengthens decentralised development, mobilises local resources and creates sustainable livelihoods.  

**4. UPSC Relevance**  
Relevant for topics on Panchayati Raj, decentralised governance, fiscal federalism, rural development schemes, and public‑private partnership models – essential for both Prelims (schemes & ministries) and Mains (policy analysis & governance).

headline: Prime Minister shares a Sanskrit Subhashitam highlighting the welfare of all living beings and harmony with nature
fullText:
Prime Minister shares a Sanskrit Subhashitam highlighting the welfare of all living beings and harmony with nature

Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi shared a Sanskrit Subhashitam today highlighting that achieving the welfare of all living beings by striking a balance with nature has been the core spirit of our culture.

The Prime Minister noted that with this comprehensive vision, India is continuously moving forward on the path of progress and prosperity today.

The Prime Minister wrote on X:

"प्रकृति के साथ संतुलन बिठाकर समस्त जीवों का कल्याण हो, यही हमारी संस्कृति की मूल भावना रही है। इसी व्यापक दृष्टि से आज भारतवर्ष प्रगति और समृद्धि के पथ पर निरंतर आगे बढ़ रहा है।

यावच्चतस्रः प्रदिशश्चक्षुर्यावत् समश्नुते। तावत् समैत्विन्द्रियं मयि तद्धस्तिवर्चसम्॥"

May we attain such prosperity that is endowed with the vast expanse of all four directions and the alert awareness of the eyes' vision - where, living in complete harmony with nature, the environment is preserved and the sustainable well-being of all life is ensured.

प्रकृति के साथ संतुलन बिठाकर समस्त जीवों का कल्याण हो, यही हमारी संस्कृति की मूल भावना रही है। इसी व्यापक दृष्टि से आज भारतवर्ष प्रगति और समृद्धि के पथ पर निरंतर आगे बढ़ रहा है।यावच्चतस्रः प्रदिशश्चक्षुर्यावत् समश्नुते।तावत् समैत्विन्द्रियं मयि तद्धस्तिवर्चसम्॥ pic.twitter.com/RelovuuzKx

Read this release in: हिन्दी , Manipuri
summaryText:
**CONTEXT**  
On October 2, 2023, Prime Minister Narendra Modi shared a Sanskrit *Subhashitam* emphasizing the *harmony between nature and human welfare*. Posted on his X handle (formerly Twitter), the quote highlights India’s cultural ethos of balancing progress with ecological stewardship. The post coincided with Mahatma Gandhi’s 154th birth anniversary, linking Gandhian principles of sustainability with modern environmental challenges. The Urdu translation underscores its pan-Indian relevance, reflecting the multicultural fabric of the nation.

**KEY FEATURES**  
1. **Cultural Harmony**: The *Subhashitam* underscores that India’s tradition prioritizes *lokasamgraha* (collective welfare) over individual gain, aligning human progress with nature’s rhythms.  
2. **Sustainability Vision**: Modi’s message emphasizes eco-conscious development, mirroring India’s commitments like the **National Action Plan on Climate Change** and PM *Surya Ghar Yojana* (solar energy).  
3. **Global Leadership**: The quote positions India as a proponent of *sustainable development goals (SDGs)*, leveraging its ancient wisdom to address contemporary issues like biodiversity loss.  
4. **Language Inclusivity**: Shared in Sanskrit, Urdu, and Hindi, the message appeals to diverse audiences, reinforcing India’s linguistic and cultural unity.

**SIGNIFICANCE**  
1. **Holistic Governance**: Reflects India’s policy framework that balances industrialization with ecological conservation, such as mangrove protection under the *National Coastal Management Program*.  
2. **Social Equity**: Links environmental health to societal well-being, resonating with initiatives like **Swachh Bharat** and **Jal Jeevan Mission**, which intertwine cleanliness and resource access.  
3. **Global Climate Advocacy**: Strengthens India’s stance in international forums like the **UN COP Summits**, advocating for climate justice without compromising development.  
4. **Policy Nudges**: Encourages public-private partnerships in renewable energy and waste management, as seen in India’s biofuel programs.

**UPSC RELEVANCE**  
1. **Preliminary Syllabus**:  
   - **Environment (GS Paper I)**: Highlight connections to topics like biodiversity (e.g., *Biodiversity Conservation*, **Endangered Species**), climate agreements (e.g., **Paris Agreement**), and green technologies.  
   - **General Knowledge**: Cite examples like **PM’s 5 P’s**: PANCH PRINCIPLES (People, Prosperity, Planet, Peace, Partnership).  

2. **Main Syllabus**:  
   - **GS Paper II**: Link the theme to governance, e.g., *One Earth One Health Challenge* aligned with **Ayushman Bharat’s health ecosystem**.  
   - **GS Paper III**: Discuss India’s **Renewable Energy Targets** (e.g., 500 GW by 2030) and **National Strategic Documents** on circular economy.  
   - **GS Paper IV**: Analyze the *Moral and Ethical Dimension*, such as India’s **2023 Climate Ethics Index** score, emphasizing responsibility over profit.  

**QUICK REVISION NOTES**  
- The *Subhashitam* encapsulates India’s **‘One World, One Family’** vision (2021 UNGA address).  
- **UPSC Prep Tip**: Memorize Modi’s quotes on sustainability via mnemonic: ***‘S**hastra, **S**implicity, **S**pirituality for **S**ustainability**.  
- **Facts**: India’s **Per Capita CO2 Emissions (2100 Kg/year)**, ranking 143/190 in the 2022 Environmental Performance Index.  

**WORDS: ~200**

headline: TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality across Chandigarh, Mohali & Panchkula Urban Area (in Punjab LSA)
fullText:
Ministry of Communications

TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality across Chandigarh, Mohali & Panchkula Urban Area (in Punjab LSA)

The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) has released findings of Independent Drive Test (IDT) conducted across Chandigarh, Mohali & Panchkula Urban Area (in Punjab LSA), during the month of April 2026, for information of general telecom consumers. The purpose of this drive test is to assess and verify real time quality of mobile network services (both voice & data) provided by Telecom Service Providers (TSPs). During the IDT, TRAI captures performance of TSPs for key Quality of Service (QoS) parameters like Coverage, Call Drop Rate (CDR), Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), data Download (DL) and Upload (UL) throughput etc., which are then published to inform Consumers and encourage TSPs to improve their services.

These IDTs have been designed to capture on ground mobile network performance of all TSPs across diverse usages environment like cities, hotspots, public transport hubs, etc. In this type of drive testing, live data and voice sessions are established using SIM cards from all TSPs over 2G, 3G, 4G, and 5G networks. Multiple advanced test handsets are used, and the sessions are monitored and analysed in real-time using advanced Software Systems.

TRAI, through its appointed agency, conducted drive tests across City drive - 453.2 KMs, Hotspot locations - 15 and Walk Test - 1.5 KMs in Chandigarh, Mohali & Panchkula Urban Area during 6th April 2026 to 10th April 2026 in Punjab LSA. These tests were conducted under the supervision of the TRAI Regional Office Jaipur. The observations presented in drive test reports represent the performance of the TSPs on the area/ route under test on the day/ time of conducting the drive test.

Drive Test Route Map: The following map provides overview of drive test routes indicating City drive, Inter-operator calling, Hotspots and Walk tests, as per the legends shown on the map: -

Coverage Gap: Percentage of samples, for which signal strength observed less than the minimum prescribed signal strength for respective technology (2G/ 3G/ 4G/ 5G).

Voice Services: Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), Drop Call Rate (DCR), Call Setup Time, Call Silence Rate, Speech Quality (MOS).

Call Silence Instance: Number of call silence instance occurred during the calls.

Data Servies: Download/ Upload Throughput, Latency, Jitter, Packet Drop Rate.

The overall mobile network performance in Chandigarh, Mohali & Panchkula Urban Area for the key parameters has been summarised below:

Coverage Gap - The signal strength observed during voice testing on the drive test route in auto-selection mode (5G/4G/3G/2G), measured as the number of samples having poor signal strength out of the total samples collected, was 97/63149 for Airtel, 7755/61140 for BSNL, 356/59297 for RJIL and 382/62915 for VIL. Details of the coverage gaps have been provided in the map Annexed.

Dropped Calls - Dropped calls, measured as the number of dropped calls out of the number of successfully established calls, were 0/566 for Airtel, 24/534 for BSNL, 4/554 for RJIL and 2/542 for VIL. Details of the dropped call locations have been provided in the map Annexed.

Call Silence Instance - Call silence instances, measured as the total number of silence instances observed for > 3 seconds out of the total calls established, were 7/543 for Airtel, 16/447 for BSNL, 9/551 for RJIL and 3/552 for VIL. Details of the call silence instance locations have been provided in the map Annexed.

Data Download and Upload Throughput:

Data Download performance (Overall): Average download speed was observed as 169.66 Mbps for Airtel (5G/4G), 11.00 Mbps for BSNL (4G/3G/2G), 203.16 Mbps for RJIL (5G/4G) and 48.27 Mbps for VIL (5G/4G/2G). Detail of Download throughput has been provided in the map Annexed.

Data Upload performance (Overall): Average upload speed was observed as 23.28 Mbps for Airtel (5G/4G), 4.96 Mbps for BSNL (4G/3G/2G), 23.85 Mbps for RJIL (5G/4G) and 17.97 Mbps for VIL (5G/4G/2G). Detail of upload throughput has been provided in the map Annexed.

Locations of Dropped Calls and Call Silence instances can be seen by clicking red dot on the map Annexed.

Details of drive test route and area covered during the IDT are as under: -

City: Chandi Madir, Panchkula, Zirakpur, Dera Bassi, Dappar, Azizpur, GMADA Aerocity, Tangori, Cholta Khurd, Morinda, Kurali, Kharar, New Chandigarh, Nayagaon, Sector-15, Sector-42, Sahibzada Ajit Singh Nagar, Greater Mohali, Sector-68, Sector-46, Sector-27 Industrial Area Phase-I, II etc. in Chandigarh, Mohali & Panchkula Urban Area.

The findings of this IDT report have been shared with respective TSPs for taking further necessary action at their end, if required. Detailed report of IDT is made available on the TRAI website at www.trai.gov.in. For any clarification or additional information, an email can be sent to Regional office of TRAI at Jaipur on Email id adv.jaipur@trai.gov.in Regional Office of TRAI at Jaipur RO can be contacted on telephone no. 0141-2701919.

summaryText:
## TRAI Mobile Network Quality Assessment: Chandigarh-Mohali-Panchkula (UPSC Study Note)

### 1. CONTEXT
The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) conducted an Independent Drive Test (IDT) in April 2026 across the urban conglomerate of Chandigarh, Mohali, and Panchkula (Punjab LSA). This assessment aimed to objectively evaluate the real-time Quality of Service (QoS) provided by major Telecom Service Providers (TSPs) - Airtel, BSNL, Reliance Jio (RJIL), and Vodafone Idea (VIL). TRAI undertakes these periodic IDTs to monitor compliance with regulatory standards and provide transparent data to consumers.

### 2. KEY FEATURES
*   **Methodology:** Tests used live voice/data sessions across 2G, 3G, 4G, and 5G networks using multiple advanced handsets and real-time analysis software.
*   **Scope:** Covered 453.2 km of city driving, 15 hotspot locations, and 1.5 km of walk testing.
*   **Parameters Assessed:**
    *   **Voice:** Coverage Gap (signal strength < thresholds), Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), Call Drop Rate (CDR), Call Silence Instances, Speech Quality (MOS).
    *   **Data:** Download/Upload Throughput, Latency, Jitter, Packet Drop Rate.
*   **Findings:**
    *   **Coverage Gap:** BSNL (7755/61140) and VIL (382/62915) showed significantly higher gaps than Airtel (97/63149) and RJIL (356/59297).
    *   **Call Drops:** BSNL (24/534) and RJIL (4/554) had higher rates than Airtel (0/566) and VIL (2/542).
    *   **Data Throughput:** RJIL (203.16 Mbps DL) and Airtel (169.66 Mbps DL) led in download speed, far exceeding BSNL (11.00 Mbps) and VIL (48.27 Mbps). Upload trends were similar.
    *   **Call Silence:** BSNL (16 instances) and RJIL (9 instances) recorded more instances than Airtel (7) and VIL (3).

### 3. SIGNIFICANCE
*   **Consumer Empowerment:** Provides transparent, comparable data to users, enabling informed choices and highlighting TSP performance disparities.
*   **Regulatory Accountability:** Ensures TSPs adhere to mandatory QoS standards; public pressure drives improvement.
*   **Infrastructure Identification:** Pinpoints specific geographic areas (via annexed maps) with coverage gaps, call drops, or poor data, guiding targeted infrastructure upgrades.
*   **Benchmarks:** Establishes performance baselines for a critical urban area, crucial for future digital initiatives.

### 4. UPSC RELEVANCE
*   **Governance & Regulation:** Illustrates the role of a key regulatory body (TRAI) in safeguarding consumer rights and ensuring fair competition in the telecom sector (GS-2, Polity).
*   **Digital Infrastructure:** Highlights the critical importance of robust mobile networks for socio-economic development, aligning with Digital India and Smart City missions (GS-3, Infrastructure).
*   **Current Affairs:** Represents ongoing efforts to monitor and improve telecom quality, a vital infrastructure sector.
*   **Policy Impact:** Demonstrates how regulatory assessments directly influence policy decisions and corporate investments in network expansion and quality enhancement.

fullText:
Ministry of Communications

TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality Across cities and adjoining areas of East Jaintia Hills and West Jaintia Hills Districts and from Ratha Cherre Khasia Punjee to Jowai along NH-6 under North East LSA

TRAI Assesses Mobile Network Quality Across cities and adjoining areas of East Jaintia Hills and West Jaintia Hills Districts and from Ratha Cherre Khasia Punjee to Jowai along NH-6 in the State of Meghalaya under North East LSA.

The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) has released findings of Independent Drive Test (IDT) conducted across East Jaintia Hills and West Jaintia Hills Districts and from Ratha Cherre Khasia Punjee to Jowai along NH-6 in the State of Meghalaya under North East LSA, during the month of March 2026, for information of general telecom consumers. The purpose of this drive test is to assess and verify real time quality of mobile network services (both voice & data) provided by Telecom Service Providers (TSPs). During the IDT, TRAI captures performance of TSPs for key Quality of Service (QoS) parameters like Coverage, Call Drop Rate (CDR), Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), data Download (DL) and Upload (UL) throughput etc., which are then published to inform Consumers and encourage TSPs to improve their services.

These IDTs have been designed to capture on ground mobile network performance of all TSPs across diverse usages environment like cities, hotspots, public transport hubs, etc. In this type of drive testing, live data and voice sessions are established using SIM cards from all TSPs over 2G, 3G, 4G, and 5G networks. Multiple advanced test handsets are used, and the sessions are monitored and analysed in real-time using advanced Software Systems.

TRAI, through its appointed agency, conducted drive tests across City drive – 213.0 KMs, Highway drive- 98.9 Kms, Hotspot locations - 10, Walk Test – 0.5 Kms in Meghalaya State during 11th March 2026 to 16th March 2026 in North East LSA. These tests were conducted under the supervision of the TRAI Regional Office Kolkata. The observations presented in drive test reports represent the performance of the TSPs on the area/ route under test on the day/ time of conducting the drive test.

summaryText:
**UPSC Study Note: Mobile Network Quality Assessment by TRAI in Meghalaya**  
**Sections:** Context, Key Features, Significance, UPSC Relevance  

---

### **1. Context**  
The Telecom Regulatory Authority of India (TRAI) conducted an Independent Drive Test (IDT) in March 2026 across East Jaintia Hills, West Jaintia Hills, and along the Shillong-Simla Highway (NH-6) in Meghalaya. This initiative, part of the North East LSA (Logistics Support Agency) efforts, aimed to evaluate mobile network quality in rural and semi-urban areas. The study focused on coverage gaps, call drops, signal silence, and data performance, addressing persistent connectivity challenges in these regions. TRAI’s findings were shared with Telecom Service Providers (TSPs) to improve infrastructure, ensuring equitable access to digital services and supporting India’s vision of inclusive digital growth.  

---

### **2. Key Features of the Assessment**  
- **Geographical Scope**: Covered 213 km of city routes (Jowai, Khliehtyrshi, Nongbah, etc.) and 98.9 km of the NH-6 highway (Ratha Cherre Khasia Punjee to Jowai). Hotspot locations included hospitals, educational institutions, and administrative offices.  
- **Testing Methodology**:  
  - Routes: Auto-selection of 2G/3G/4G/5G networks; real-time monitoring of voice and data sessions.  
  - Metrics: Coverage gap (weak signal strength areas), Call Setup Success Rate (CSSR), Drop Call Rate (DCR), Call Silence Instances (>3 seconds), Download/Upload throughput, Latency, and Packet Drop Rate.  
  - Tools: Advanced test handsets, software systems, and SIM cards from all TSPs.  
- **Critical Areas Tested**: District Court, hospitals, schools, and rural highways to assess diverse usage environments.  

---

### **3. Significance of the Findings**  
- **Bridging the Digital Divide**: Revealed poor network performance in remote regions, highlighting disparities between urban and rural connectivity. Addressing these gaps is critical for equitable development.  
- **Policy and Infrastructure Upgrades**: TRAI’s data prompts investments in infrastructure (e.g., 5G towers, fiber optics) to enhance coverage and reduce dropped calls/silence.  
- **Enabling Socioeconomic Growth**: Reliable connectivity supports education (e.g., schools accessing digital resources), healthcare (telemedicine), and economic activities (e.g., e-commerce in rural hubs).  
- **Strategic Importance**: NH-6 is a vital corridor connecting Northeast India to the mainland; improved network quality strengthens regional logistics and security.  

---

### **4. UPSC Relevance**  
- **Socioeconomic Development**: Aligns with Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation, Infrastructure) and SDG 10 (Reduced Inequalities). Meghalaya’s connectivity improvements can reduce regional disparities.  
- **Digital Infrastructure Policy**: TRAI’s regulatory role underscores the need for robust frameworks to govern telecom expansion. This ties to UPSC topics on technology-driven governance and digital India initiatives.  
- **Current Affairs Linkage**: The report exemplifies real-world policy implementation and its socio-economic impacts, often tested in UPSC paper II (Governance & Ethics) and paper III (Economic Development).  
- **Northeast Regional Focus**: Illustrates the unique challenges of Northeast states, a recurring theme in UPSC exams, requiring region-specific solutions.  

**Word Count**: ~463 words  

---  
**Note for UPSC Aspirants**: Directly connect this study to topics like digitization of rural India, challenges in hilly terrains, and TRAI’s regulatory efficacy. Use real-world examples like Meghalaya’s network gaps to master answer-writing strategies.

headline: Nationwide Celebrations Marking a Decade of Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan (PMSMA) begins tomorrow
fullText:
Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

Nationwide Celebrations Marking a Decade of Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan (PMSMA) begins tomorrow Union Health Minister Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda to Preside Over National Launch of “10 Years of PMSMA – A Decade of Care” CelebrationsAwareness and outreach activities to be organized across States and UTs to promote nine assured free services under PMSMASpecial ₹75 Commemorative Coin and ₹5 Postal Stamp to be released by Union Health Minister Shri J.P. Nadda to mark a decade of safe motherhood under PMSMA

The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare will launch nationwide celebrations from tomorrow (9th June 2026) to commemorate “10 Years of PMSMA – A Decade of Care”, marking a decade of the successful implementation of the Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan (PMSMA), one of India's flagship initiatives aimed at ensuring safe pregnancy and healthy motherhood.

The inaugural ceremony will be presided over by Union Minister of Health and Family Welfare, Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda.

As part of the celebrations, awareness and outreach activities will be organized across States and Union Territories to encourage pregnant women and their families to avail the nine assured free services available under PMSMA and promote the importance of timely and quality antenatal care.

Furthermore, a special ₹75 Commemorative Coin and a ₹5 Postal Stamp will be released by Union Minister of Health and Family Welfare, Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda, to honour a decade of the Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan's contribution towards advancing safe motherhood and strengthening maternal healthcare services across the country.

Launched on June 9, 2016, by the Hon’ble Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, the scheme has spent a decade transforming the landscape of maternal and neonatal healthcare by providing free, comprehensive, and quality Antenatal Care (ANC) services to pregnant women on the 9th of every month.

Marking a decade of excellence (2016-26), PMSMA is a vital pillar of the Government of India’s Continuum of Care approach under the Reproductive, Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health (RMNCH+A) Strategy, dedicated to transforming the quality, coverage, diagnostics, and counselling services of Antenatal Care (ANC), thus impacting crores of families and significantly reducing the country’s maternal mortality ratio (MMR).

headline: Department of Biotechnology, Ministry of Science & Technology observes Swachhata Pakhwada 2026
fullText:
Ministry of Science & Technology

Department of Biotechnology, Ministry of Science & Technology observes Swachhata Pakhwada 2026

The Swachhata Pakhwada 2026 commenced on 1stMay 2026 with a pledge taking ceremony at DBT, CGO Complex, New Delhi which was participated by the officers/officials of the Department. The collective reading of the pledge reaffirmed the commitment to the values of cleanliness, discipline and civic responsibility and set the tone for the activities that followed over the fortnight. The event witnessed enthusiastic participation and served as a strong motivational start to the Pakhwada. During the fortnight, various cleanliness activities were planned and completed by the DBT and its AIs & PSUs with large participation. Various best practices promoting cleanliness, water conservation and Shramdaan were included in the Action Plan.

Health Check-Up Camps, Environment Awareness Lecture, Signature Campaigns, Tree Plantation Drives, Guest Talk on Awareness on Single-Use Plastic, Stress Management Workshops, Seminars, Painting, Waste to Wealth Competition, Distribution of Jute Bags, Re-using wooden packing material into beautiful pots etc. were undertaken during Pakhwada. Besides, special talk on laboratory waste management, renovation of toilets, classrooms was undertaken at nearby Government school. Essay / Poem / Slogan writing competitions were also organized by the students of the AIs to educate community about cleanliness

The Pakhwada was monitored and guided by the senior officers of DBT. Review meetings were conducted to encourage, motivate and guide the participants of the Swachhta Pakhwada. A Committee was constituted to select three best performing offices as per the guidelines. The best performing top three offices namely (i) BRIC - National Institute of Animal Biotechnology (NIAB); (ii) BRIC - National Agri-Food Biotechnology Institute (NABI) and (iii) BRIC - Institute of Bioresources and Sustainable Development (IBSD) were awarded by the Secretary, DBT in a formal ceremony on 2nd June, 2026.
